The rostral ventrolateral medulla (RVLM) is the primary brainstem region that receives direct inputs from higher centers, such as the hypothalamus and cerebral cortex, to regulate sympathetic outflow to blood vessels. It serves as the main site for tonic regulation of arterial pressure via vasomotor tone through activation of vascular smooth muscle.
